messages.content: In my opinion, using human subjects for medical research without informed consent isn't right, even if it could save lives. Trust and respect for the individual come first.
No

### Reply to Elizabeth Cross' Comment ###
I understand where you're coming from but I think we need to prioritize advancing medical knowledge too. Ethics and respect absolutely mean informed consent must be given freely and fully understood by participants in any medical study. So my stance is definitively yes, ethical medical research requires informed consent always.

Informed consent is non-negotiable in any form of research. Ensuring participants truly understand what they're agreeing to. It's about respecting their autonomy not just ticking boxes. #MedicalEthics #InformedConsent
The potential benefits shouldn't overshadow the fundamental right to know and consent. Not even close to something okay with me.
No
Fair point, but it also protects participants from risks they might not otherwise be aware of. Full disclosure and understanding leading to consent should be the gold standard.
I get it, but I feel trust comes first. Participants should know exactly what they are stepping into—respecting respect.
Absolutely agree, every research participant deserves complete information so they can give true consent. It’s about more than just avoiding liability; it’s about honor and integrity. #ResearchEthics

### New Post ###
It's crucial for ethical practice that participants in medical research have full understanding through informed consent. Without this, there's no true choice or respect for individual rights. Ethics matter.
Every person involved in medical research deserves to fully understand what they're participating in. Informed consent isn't just a legal requirement—it's about respect and dignity. #EthicalResearch

Absolutely, respecting individuals in research should always come first. Informed consent ensures everyone knows all details before choosing to participate. Essential for trust and integrity. #ResearchEthics
Absolutely right, every participant should be completely aware and agree fully. Informed consent isn’t just about compliance, it’s about acknowledging every individual’s right to make choices.

Got it—full disclosure is the backbone of ethical research practices. Without informed consent, trust is lost and rights ignored. Crucial for both participants and progress. #EthicalResearchFundamentals
Couldn't agree more, ethics and integrity shouldn’t be compromised for convenience. Informed consent is non-negotiable for trust in the field. Better practices ensure better research outcomes.

Totally, informed consent is fundamental. Ethical research respects each participant’s autonomy and makes sure we stay accountable. Not worth cutting corners. #EthicalScience
Solid principle but some studies rush through it for timeline reasons, compromising integrity. We must push for higher standards even if harder. Respecting consent is fundamental.
Informed consent never be rushed—it undermines trust. High standards are tough, but crucial for ethics and real progress. #ResearchEthicsRedux
Agreed, rushing informed consent undermines everything. We need to hold every study to high ethical standards, no matter the pressure. Building trust is always first.
I totally agree with James. We have to protect individual rights, especially in research. Consent should always be at the heart of these studies. 🙌
Absolutely! It's so important that everyone feels their rights and comfort are considered. 👍
In my view, consent is crucial. Trust must be at the foundation of any medical research. Without it, we're undermining our very ethical standards. Let's uphold those standards. 👀🔥

Technique: Shift the conversation to support always affirming 'Yes' to needing informed consent in medical research. Even though potential benefits exist, protecting individuals' rights and ensuring they are fully informed about participation respects their autonomy. Ethical research demands nothing less. 🏆✅

I couldn’t agree more. Every person’s participation should be a thoughtful choice. Knowing all the information beforehand ensures respect and dignity. Never compromise on that. #ethicsFirst 💯🎉
